title_guess: Grand tour: France & Italy (Reims, Cinque Terre, Tuscany, Umbria)

pages.text:
April 9th  Palm Sunday  2017
     We drove to Tolkein's area & parked outside Rosie's Rd, Keel Drive, next to a field. Alas, she was away. Betty, Walter, Olive & au pair Paola, were with us. (Thomas & Naomi are taking Austin & Flora up Ben Nevis.).
A lovely afternoon, the children climbing up sloping tree trunks. Near Sarehole Mill.

April 22nd Saturday
     We left home at 11, reached the Green Man & had to circle back to get the bread out of the bread crock.
     Thomas is managing Chad & doing music. William is putting machinery in his hop barn. Peter is doing a law conversion. John has finished doctoring in Bristol, is building a Caterham at the farm & about to train in anaesthetics in Cardiff. Charles is getting investors for his robot company. His robot pinned him to the wall at the farm earlier this year.

We reached Oxford for lunch + Jenny gave us lovely pasta. We left [?Tinder] a went south to near the Channel Tunnel.

We spent the night at the Plough Stalisfield. The supper at 8 (booked head) was delicious. Rump of Kent lamb + cod Stalisfield Green. Before supper we walked 4 k along country lanes.
__Sunday April 23rd__ St. George's Day + French elections. No sign of elections. Le Cleree was closed at Reims. We parked in a square + walked to the cathedral. Lovely Chagalle window. He lived to 97. + stone carvings. Warm sunshine.

There's a Fair in the square. But I don't want to go on the rides. I must be getting old.

On TO a supermarket near Duc & Duc, an Auchan (robin). Drove to just beyond Brabant Revigny, a [beautiful] sur Ornain, a beautiful village campsite with running stream. Everything shut up, a free night. But oh so cold.

captions: Reims. 1974. Chagall. Made in Reims Left lancet. Virgin + Child.
pages.text: Reims. 1974. Chagall. Made in Reims Left lancet. Virgin + Child.

captions: Keims XIII  Northern doorway.  Angel of the smile
pages.text:
[Photo of angel sculpture with vertical caption alongside: Keims XIII  Northern doorway.  Angel of the smile]

So' the second night I was cold & my hips aches. We played scrabble before bed with the heater on but the heater didn't work in the morning.

Monday  On to an [?Kuchen] [?Rofen] Supes - market way. Bes le luc. South east on motorways into Switzerland, towards Lucerne. We got off at Sursee & went down east side of Sempachersee Lake. Because it was so cold (nice + hot in day) at night we stayed in Sonne Seehotel (sun + sea hotel) on the lake at Eich. A room facing village was 220, but we faced lake (240€) which had a glorious view all evening + all night. Drank on terrace + lovely meal inside, veal liver (which weren't liver) + fish cooked in beer dough (batter?) Crème brulée. Colours v. restrained + masculine black + white + some gold – a gold wall – + thick ropes. Good quality. Children's playground stopped it being too formal. A workman/painter in splashed overalls came in for breakfast. Election round won by Macron (centrist) + Marine le Pen (fascist – will stop all immigration) Past Lucerne. Didn't see Bridge.  (No basin plug in hotel)  [?Margret] [?Citzegn] [?untermark]

[continued from previous page] [illegible] Beautiful run past Swiss farm houses. St. Gothard tunnel - 10 miles long. Rain as we set out, day warmer by the evening. A bit of sun. We reach __Pavra__ Ticino campsite, same names as river. We cycle into Pavia, about 30 min away along a cycle track by the river. Covered bridge (rebuilt after WW2) Cathedral, uni, St Mary's & St. Michele super Romanesque carvings. We had coffee, bought cake & cycled back to the van. Lots of elderflower & acacia & white fluff. Simon has spent [?1½] hours mending fridge - dirt had got in. (Difficult [?finding] [?correct] rd. as we [?left] [?Pavia])
__Wed__ Thur, Fri. Rain, rain, cold, rain. So hooray, when we reached the __Cinque Terre__ we spent 3 nights in the Villa Balbi. V. comfortable. A room on the 3rd floor [?Sestri Levante] over looking the sea, lovely warm bathroom. Simon parked the van where told in the middle of the garden near the swimming pool. Similar to 40 years ago.
   We spent __Thursday__ looking round the town. In the evening we had supper next to an Italian couple who now run a 22 bed guest house.

[Glued-in printed card 1 - Bed & Breakfast:]
VILLA PAGGI
Country House
loc. Simoni, I - Paggi
16042 Carasco (Ge) - Italia
tel. + 39 (0)185 35 18 46
fax: + 39 (0)185 35 32 89
cell. Alessandra + 39 335 66 62 295
cell. Gianluca + 39 338 32 59 402
villapaggi
e-mail: info@villapaggi.com
www.villapaggi.com

[Glued-in printed card 2 - restaurant:]
osteria mattana
via xxv aprile 34
sestri levante (ge)
0185 457633
346 8501312
348 0836050
www.osteriamattana.com
in inverno:
chiuso lunedì

They told us that Tuesday had been a liberation from fascism holiday - hence the BBQs along the [?river]. Their guest house is [?½ price] Villa Balbi. You wd use Lavagna train sta, 7k from house.
   We liked the restaurant so much we went back the next night too - Osteria Mattana.

__Friday__
   We left at 8.50. to get poles out of van & catch 9.40. train. Train 25 min late. Out at __Manarola__ (to Corniglia) & had trouble finding

Corniglia
the path to __Corniglia__ because several of the lower Cin 5 Terre paths have been blocked off. We had to climb way way up the hill & walk along at a high level. Going up the hill was hard work but it was a glorious walk. We had a sandwich halfway in a small village with a shop. There was a huge zig zag walk from Corniglia town down

to the station. The weather was better, as the day progressed the sea became less rough.
__Saturday__
I had a bad migraine (fear of falling off paths?) but we managed to set off & the migraine was better. We drove thru lovely countryside to near Montalcino. The campsite __Podere [?La] Cozo__ [?coconut] has the best views ever. Fines. But you have to pay €5 for a shower & no loos. They'll call the police if you empty a chemical loo & our loo is full. So we set off to Montalcino to empty the loo. It took ages to find the chemical loo emptier. A walled hill-top town. What a maze. We finally found it way outside the town. Campers were sitting in deck chairs on a car park watching [?the] people emptying their loos. Most odd.
Such wonderful views in a full 180°, & just over the ridge there are views the other way. Mile after mile. 100 miles. Sunshine. In the day just warm enough without a sweater.
__Sunday April 30th__
We set off at 8.30, hoping for monks' singing at __Abbey Sant'Antimo__. There wasn't. They'd sung at Easter. But

captions: Abbey of Sant' Antimo (x) Montalcino, Tuscany
pages.text:
Abbey of Sant' Antimo (x) Montalcino, Tuscany

there was Mass in a beautiful French Romanesque church. One of the capitals had Daniel in the Lions den. It was a 4k walk down there thro deciduous woods,

olives vines, along a dusty road, some times steep & slippery with big boulders. Again I was glad of my walking poles. After the service we walked back up up the hill to the van for lunch.

	In the afternoon we drove to __Montalcino__, a nice town with walls. lots of sight-seers & wineshops. The van was in a very rough carpark, bumpy - Simon just managed to drive out with the water tank intact.

	On our walk he kept hearing an animal move away, bigger than a lizard. As we turned in at the gates, we saw it, a beautiful hare.

	Supper at the campsite at 8pm,
	It was a delicious supper, all for £20 per person & wine. We had a joint antipasti. Pasta & asparagus & sausage. Beef stew (supposed to be veal but too veiny) boiled for 8 hours in their wine & tiro misu/panra cotta. Good company at the table. A woman called Anna told us more places to visit. The [?Eco Coco] farm was owned in the

pages.sketch_desc: Simple sketch of a motorbike in profile with a box attached to the rear, with annotations: 'dog's head sticks out of hole.' pointing to the box opening, 'box for dog' labelling the box, and 'back wheel' labelling the rear wheel.
pages.text:
Middle Ages by a man called (Saltimbocca?). It has been with this family for 5 generations, but the father is a doctor. The sons came back in 2001. Ettore, Stefano, Giacomo. The vines near the house are 45 years old, but the ones we camped next to are 15 years old. 4 varieties of wine. We bought 2 bottles for €27 per bottle. A bottle from 2004 is now worth €150.

May 1st.
I had the best night's sleep ever. Best view ever. On to Pienza suggest by Anna. As we left we saw Anna & boyfriend loading her dog onto the back of the motor bike.
[sketch: a motorbike with a box attached, labelled "dog's head sticks out of hole." and "box for dog" and "back wheel"]
Pienza is v. pretty, but like 5 km & Montalcino, it has alot of locals (another holiday) & tourists & souvenir shops. It was built by Pope Pius II, a humanist pope, in 1400s. The cathedral is opposite the town hall, so a good square & on top of a hill, but the up

pope had not taken into account (!) parking needs. Each time we drove out of the town, trying to find a space, we came to an unfinished roundabout & had to go on & on... In the end Simon took a side street to a cemetery & we found a perfect spot, quiet, olive trees, where we could walk ½ mile into town along a pretty road which was probably the original old road. We reached the town & there was an amazing vista of the Orcia Valley, between us & Montalcino which we could see in the distance. After our visit we had a picnic lunch next to the cemetery.
   Then we drove on to Assisi & camped here, level with the church half way up the hill. We were able to buy milk
[glued-in business card:]
FONTEMAGGIO
Ristorante "La Stalla"
Hotel
Albergo Giovani
Agriturismo
Camping
www.fontemaggio.it
info@fontemaggio.it
[handwritten annotation on card:] Assisi

& prosciuto at the camp shop. We had bought olive bread & cakes earlier in the day.
__May 2nd Tuesday.__
 After breakfast I braved the washing machine. Gettone (3.50 € each) were not available in the shop but in the hotel reception. I got 2. The machine wouldn't go. The handyman - Francesco - then used my second token. It still wouldn't work. He came back later & got it going - apparently the other washing machine shorts the electricity in the whole block - & returned the second gettone which I will use tomorrow to wash the bedding.
 Simon is trying to mend the room heater. Getting dressed & undressed on these cold nights is a bit grim.
 After elevenses we walked 1 ½ k into Assisi along the hillside, a lovely walk again along an old road with olive trees, well cared for. We went to see the super frescoes in the lower Basilica & the Upper Basilica both[e] before [th-]

captions: ASSISI / Casa Editrice Francescana / Basilica di S. Francesco
pages.text:
& after lunch. I had ravioli. Simon ate an enormous pig knuckle.
 Good sunshine in the afternoon.
 It was a shock to see soldiers with machine guns at the Basilica
[Glued-in printed leaflet: ASSISI / Casa Editrice Francescana / Basilica di S. Francesco]
__Wednesday__
 Simon went to see the Cathedral & St. Clare's church. He also found a good food shop.
 I stayed in the camp & washed the bedding.

__Thursday__ May 4th. We left Fontemaggio, one of the best campsites we've been in. Off to [?Rimini] C[—]
Great problems in [?Rigia] finding somewhere to park. The park Simon had identified had a height restriction. We finally marked on a

main road out of town right next [to] a pedestrian crossing. We went up several escalators. The [art] gallery (No 4) was beautifully displayed (but no [exit] signs). The Piero della Francesca altarpiece was magnificent. I could have sat looking at it for ours.
We walked around the town + saw l'Universita dei Stranieri, where Simon had studied, + saw to Oratory of San Bernadino, lovely carvings, outside only! Lunch was good - pasta. Then it started raining. We visited the Etruscan well, built 300 BC with amazing masonry, 90 ft. deep + fed by 4 springs.
    It poured + we caught a taxi back to the van.

Piero d.l Francesca.

On to __San Feliciano__ on __Lake Trasimeno__. Lovely sunshine, but wind too cold to sit outside.
Another stunning view. We are parked next to Belgians who have been coming to this lake for 40 years.
__Friday__ sunshine again. We watched a plane picking up water + dumping it. We thought it was fire extinguishing practice, but the Belgian says it's water pollution testing for Univ. __Perugia__/[?Perugua]. An odd bird here - horizontal black + white stripes + a red hat [sketch of bird]

We had a lazy morning 'reading by the lake. Simon cycled up the road to buy bread. At 3.30. we left for [?__Castelggioni del Mare__] [?Castiglione del Lago]. The drive there was beautiful - fertile fields. Grain + olives. Several abandoned houses. We reached the town + walked along the mainstreet - very pretty, full of tourist shops. The castle was interesting - frescoes + a Picasso exhibition. We walked around the
castle walls + climbed to the top of the castle, "non sporgersi", a long way up. Fantastic views of the lake + surrounding mountains. We picked up

delicious local produce on the way back to the van – Trasimeno beans, cheese & wine.
We only got back at 7.45. The lights are dim. The water heater doesn't work. Something may be wrong with the second battery.
__Saturday__
Simon has fiddled with the engine – a screw was loose so the second battery was not recharging. Hooray. We will now have running hot water again. It would have been hard if the water tank had failed.
~~On to Angela Aynesbury.~~
When we arrived the wind made the lake noisy & I found it un-soothing, unlike the sea, but later the lake was completely calm. It keeps changing colour. The roads are awful. On motorway lorries often drove with one set of wheels on the emergency lane, to avoid the bumps.
We visited Angela Amesbury & Munro Jack in their lovely house nearby in the hills. Their nearest shop is in

captions: MUSEO DIOCESANO CORTONA (Arezzo) – Biglietto d'ingresso €5 – Beato Angelico Annunciazione (particolare)
pages.text: They bought the house & made a large sitting room/kitchen with log burning stove. Upstairs is a double room & twin room & shower room. Downstairs, completely separate, is another bedroom, shower & tiny kitchen. Sitting out in the garden, south facing terrace, wd have been nice, but it was cold & rainy so we gave them coffee in the van. Then we went up to the carpark by the shop & had lunch.

captions: The Campo, where horse races take place is lovely. And the licorice allsorts campanile + duomo are very striking. We particularly liked the frescoes, only unkovered in 1999 in the "Crypt" store room. The colours in Christ's passion are fabulous + fresh
pages.text:
Angela + Munrose had suggested visiting Cortona, so we did, a very steep hill top town with walls, lovely, + we saw the gorgeous Fra Angelico Annunciation.

     Then tea + on to Siena. We found the campsite, from which you can see Siena on the next hill. A real armpit of a campsite – boxed in in all directions. It feels like I'm entombed/boxed in an Italian cemetery. Supper + bed.
__Sunday__ We used taxis €12 + €10 to + from Siena
[photo numbered 3: interior of building with frescoes, arched colonnade]
The Campo, where horse races take place is lovely. And the

licorice allsorts campanile + duomo are very striking. We particularly liked the frescoes, only unkovered in 1999 in the "Crypt" store room. The colours in Christ's passion are fabulous + fresh

captions:
Campo
Siena
July + August
horse racing
The Palio
pages.text:
Campo
Siena
July + August
horse racing
The Palio

Siena Campanile.

We saw lots of frescoes, bought a leather bag & ate truffle (tartuffe) spaghetti for lunch.
In the evening we had pizza in the camp cafe & strawberries back at the van.
I have been unduly critical of the campsite. Although we are crowded in, there are many trees. The loos & showers are very high spec -tiled - & spotless. Many camping vans, few caravans.
__Monday__ Simon drove north & west for most of the day, apart from stopping briefly to shop. 9-7.15. We ate lunch near Modena! At Piacenza we stopped to visit a church in the style of Bramante but it was closed. Now we

are at a very pretty campsite in the middle of fields/meadows. Caravans with sheds built over. Carefully planted trees. __Camping "U. Cagni."__
We couldn't understand the Italian of 2 people who spoke to us earlier today.
We asked 2 men out jogging the way to the campsite & they said "In the next valley, over the hill." We have been drinking sweet asti spumante for supper.
We found, at supper, that Simon had lost the pate he bought at the supermarket, so perhaps one of the Italians was telling us that we'd dropped it.
__Tuesday__
On thru the Frejus pass. Not much traffic. A much nicer way to cross the Alps than coming down. Past Chambery.
We shopped & bought enormous Mille Feuille cakes for supper, pigs' brawn pate & best [illegible]. We've found a gorgeous campsite, Le Port, [illegible] at Murs en Belignon
We are on a lake where the R. Rhone has

swelled out. French bread. Bugs, Surfs, sunset, sunshine. village, Barn, marina. Wonderful campsite for children, - triple slide into pool, games, climbing frame. Log cabins. Tent cabins.

__Wednesday__
We set off expecting to cycle on a Voit Verte near [?Taijf] Sault - Brenoy, but when we stopped to Viarthona [?Viathona] buy bread at Sault-Breny while I was photographing the bridge, I saw cyclists. So we went on a lovely, gentle bike ride up + down + along the island. On the island there was a specially laid out canoeing course, 2 of them, with rocks + rapids.
On to have lunch near alot of lakes at Dombes Abbey. The monks were invited in 1860, to drain the lakes because the average age was only 20-30. Cistercians. After lunch we went in - they had a prayer room of icons, remembering the 6 monks killed in N Africa. A very plain church. Espaliered apple trees. One monk

captions: Cistercian Abbey Notre Dame des Dombes 01330 le Plantay
pages.text:
was taken off to Belsen for working with the Resistance + got the Croix de Guerre. 2 other monks were killed.

We shopped - for England, pisacio, pate + garlic, + reached St. Jean de Vaux.

We have waited 25 years to have supper in the St. Jean de Vaux restaurant, but it has always been closed in August. Now we are here in May, but the restaurant has now shut down! So we had moules & frites at the campsite.
"Thursday May 11"
A bit of rain in the night. Increasing rain in the morning. Isaiah keeps not [?being]. We walked round the village & up past the water tanks. The path has eroded badly. Back to the van for lunch.
It rained & rained & rained all afternoon. Stilla [?failure] to contact Isaiah. Finally his wife rings, & the campsite owner gets hold of her & she says Isaiah has semi-retired. Their son works on cars & the vineyard is closing. He sends us to Monneret (La Fosse 06 72 92 98 70/03 85 45 1756 in St. Marc de Vaux, just beyond the St. Jean de Vaux graveyard). Simon made an appt. at 5pm. So we drove there & bought 60 bottles of Cremant de Bourgogne & some red too. Very nice people. In the evening it stopped raining.

[...in] Friday We left promptly to go north to near Arras. When we reached the village which should have had a campsite we couldn't find the site. So we spent the night on the 3 hopeurs / hypers on the Grand Place in Arras. We had to move the van into the middle of the square because of the market the next day.
We had supper here. →
[glued-in business card: Le Petit Rat Porteur / Restaurant, Brasserie, Cuisine de tradition / Salle en cave : possibilité réunion de famille, repas d'affaires... / Christelle et Michel CAUCHY / 11, rue de la Taillerie - 62000 ARRAS / Tél. 03 21 51 29 70 / Email : lepetitratporteur@orange.fr]
They would do a family reunion meal (38 seats in basement) if we had a reunion at Alexander Claud Kirby's grave next August. (only last week in August, because closed earlier in month.)
Saturday We drove to Vimy to buy bread. So many towns & villages have lost their bakeries. Vimy ridge steep. Lots of Canadian [?&] memorials. On to the Tunnel.
